sections.title: Talking again before being able to purchase Bologa's blessings

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you can make Zamorak's wine from grapes.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, I've invented a blessing that invokes Zamorak's unholy power upon the grapes, so Zamorak's wine can be made from them.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: But I won't let you use it unless I think you're up to it. Also, I want 75,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And do you think I'm up to it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself again.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: It's really nice of you to take an interest...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

sections.title: Talking again after being able to purchase Bologa's blessings

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Hello again, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do those blessings work again?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: My blessing is written on scrolls. You need to carry some of the scrolls when you're harvesting grapes.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: As each fruit is severed from its stem, it begins to die. That's the moment of vulnerability when Zamorak's unholy power can infest it.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: The power consumes the scroll on which the blessing is written, so you'll need one for each grape. I suggest you take a stack of blessings with you.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: By the way, Lord Zamorak's chaotic power is quite volatile. You'll likely get a smaller harvest from your vine if you're using these blessings.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I've given the scrolls to Granddad downstairs. You can get them off him as a reward for working on his farm. The place may be a dump, but he's family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me the story about yourself again.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, okay. It's nice of you to ask...

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I am Bologa Gricoller, seeker of forbidden wisdom, wanderer of the dark pathways, thinker of the unthinkable. I can do bartending too.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I used to work at The Golden Field. That's my sister's bar. It's mostly farmhands drinking there, but you get some interesting people as well.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: See, most Hosidius people are Saradominists. My sister, she's always listening to Father Jean at the church with his goodie-goodie sermons.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: But some of us in the bar thought we'd give Zamorak a try. Like, you gotta be open-minded, right? What did Saradomin ever do for us anyway?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: So we'd meet in the bar, sharing books that the Piscarilius smugglers would bring us, learning what the Saradominists don't want us to know.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Anyway, my sister - she's a right cow, by the way - she fired me and kicked me out. Said I was bringing bad sorts to her bar.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Bad sorts, she said! Like, morality is totally subjective, right? So who's she to call us bad sorts just 'cos we're not afraid to think differently?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Anyway, I've learnt all this cool stuff about Zamorak, and being stuck in a fruit farm doesn't stop me being his loyal servant, right?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: So that's how I came to invent that Zamorakian blessing for making Zamorak's wine out of grapes from the Vinery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Granddad's fruit farm. It's a right dump. This whole corner of the city is a dump.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why are you here if it's a dump?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: My cow of a sister kicked me out of her bar. Said I was getting the place a bad name. So I'm staying here until I'm old enough to get my own place.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I want to move to Arceuus. It's properly awesome up there. I'll do bartending or something until I can learn to be a necromancer.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Anyway, why are you here? Do you want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks for the info.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, you're in the biggest dumpster of Dumpsterville. Now you know. Anyway, do you want something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, alright...

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I am Bologa Gricoller, seeker of forbidden wisdom, wanderer of the dark pathways, thinker of the unthinkable. I can do bartending too.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I used to work at The Golden Field. That's my sister's bar. It's mostly farmhands drinking there, but you get some interesting people as well.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: See, most Hosidius people are Saradominists. My sister, she's always listening to Father Jean at the church with his goodie-goodie sermons.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: But some of us in the bar thought we'd give Zamorak a try. Like, you gotta be open-minded, right? What did Saradomin ever do for us anyway?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: So we'd meet in the bar, sharing books that the Piscarilius smugglers would bring us, learning what the Saradominists don't want us to know.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Anyway, my sister - she's a right cow, by the way - she fired me and kicked me out. Said I was bringing bad sorts to her bar.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Bad sorts, she said! Like, morality is totally subjective, right? So who's she to call us bad sorts just 'cos we're not afraid to think differently?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Anyway, I've learnt all this cool stuff about Zamorak, and being stuck in a fruit farm doesn't stop me being his loyal servant, right?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: In fact I've invented a Zamorakian blessing that works on fruit! You can use it to make Zamorak's wine out of grapes from the Vinery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me to make Zamorak's wine?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I'll let you have access to the Zamorakian blessing I invented, so you can invoke Zamorak's unholy power upon the grapes... if I think you're up to it.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I'll want 75,000 coins too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And do you think I'm up to it?

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Nah, I bet you couldn't even bless an unholy symbol - you're not going to get very far with my grape blessing.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Not likely - I can see from your kit that you're supporting other gods. Dress Zamorakian if you want to impress me. And I'll still want 75,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: You don't look much like a Zamorakian to me. Dress Zamorakian if you want my help. And I'll still want 75,000 coins.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, I guess you'll do. But I want 75,000 coins. One day I'll need to buy my own house, y'know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe another time.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Yeah, I guess you'll do. But I want 75,000 coins. One day I'll need to buy my own house, y'know!

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Here's how it works... I'll write the blessing on scrolls. You need to carry some of the scrolls when you're harvesting grapes.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: As each fruit is severed from its stem, it begins to die. That's the moment of vulnerability when Zamorak's unholy power can infest it.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: The power consumes the scroll on which the blessing is written, so you'll need one for each grape. I suggest you take a stack of blessings with you.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: By the way, Lord Zamorak's chaotic power is quite volatile. You'll likely get a smaller harvest from your vine if you're using these blessings.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: I'll give the scrolls to Granddad downstairs. You can get them off him as a reward if you work on his farm. Hey, it's a dump, but he's family. So, do you accept?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, no thanks.

lines.speaker: Bologa
lines.text: Meh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll leave you to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.
